text:
, first name also spelt Catherine Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood, English version, Episode 38 credits: Catherine - Kate Oxley. or Katherine Volume 10: Journey to Ishbal English DVD Episode 37 English "Additional Cast" credits Kate Bornemann as "Katherine". and sometimes given the middle name Ariel or middle initial L., is the fourth daughter and youngest child of the illustrious Armstrong Family and the youngest sister of Major General Olivier Armstrong and Major Alex Louis Armstrong.

Name

Cathrine has had more official variations of her name than potentially any other character in the Fullmetal Alchemist franchise. Her Japanese name キャスリン (Kyasurin) is a Japanese translation of a very common name in several languages which has several valid variations, although the only officially used translations have been Cathrine, Catherine, and Katherine. 

Her middle name, エル (Eru), is significantly less common by comparison, but is usually officially translated as meaning Elle, an uncommon English name which borrows its spelling from the French pronoun for "she", elle. It has also been translated as L., making it an initial for her middle name. エル (Eru) is the pronunciation of the English letter L when spoken in Japanese, however if it were truly meant to be an initial it's more likely her name would have been written as キャスリン・L・アームストロング in Japanese rather than as "エル". 

Finally, the Chuang Yi English version opts for a completely different translation of her middle name, instead making her middle name Ariel. Why this change was made is unknown, but it could have been a mixture of Elle being uncommon at the time and "Ariel" normally being アリエル (Arieru) in Japanese, sharing the last two characters with Cathrine's middle name. Ariel also could have been chosen as Ariel and Katherine are both used in French (although it should be noted that Catherine is more commonly used in French than Katherine, which is what the Chuang Yi version uses).

Currently, in English media "Catherine" is the most prominent spelling of her first name, and "Elle" is the most prominent spelling of her middle name, however Viz's English manga is the only version which uses both simultaneously. "Cathrine Elle Armstrong", on the other hand, is the most prominent English spelling of her name from Japanese media .

Following is a list of all the officially used names for Cathrine, starting with instances of her full name being given followed by instances where her middle name/initial is absent.
Cathrine Elle Armstrong is used by her 2003 anime's official character reference sheet, which is reinforced by being used as her Japanese name under "Original Japanese Credits" on English DVD credits. 
Catherine Elle Armstrong is used by Viz's English manga 
Katherine Elle Armstrong is used by the Trading Card Game, based off the 2003 anime. 
Katherine Ariel Armstrong is used by Chuang Yi's English manga. 
Catherine L. Armstrong is used by the English version of the game Dual Sympathy and the DVD subtitles of Episode 37. 
Katherine is given as her first name only as her English name in the English credits of Episode 37. 
Catherine is given as her first name only in the English credits of Episode 38. 

Appearance

Being a part of the Armstrong Family, Cathrine has many of their distinct traits, including being fair-skinned with blonde hair and a single strand sticking out in the middle of her forehead. According to Alex Louis Armstrong in Episode 37, Cathrine also "the Armstrong" eyelashes. Apart from that, unlike the imposing and/or powerful looking members of her family, Cathrine is a dainty and beautiful young girl with a slender yet noticeably curvaceous and well-endowed frame. Her hair reaches waist-length with shoulder-length hair strands hanging on either side, and she has sea-green (blue in Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood) eyes.

Personality

While a meek and soft-spoken young girl, Cathrine's demeanor and delicate appearance belie an inherited strength that allows her to lift objects like pianos above her head with one hand. Cathrine is shy by nature, but she has stated that she prefers the company of men who are as muscular and strong as her elder brother Alex Louis Armstrong. This may be an effect of how the Armstrong family values muscular strength and military backgrounds.

History

Cathrine first appears in the Bonus Chapter: The Second Lieutenant Goes to Battle! (which is later adapted into Episode 37 of the 2003 anime series) where she is formally introduced to Second Lieutenant Jean Havoc, who has been forced to end a budding relationship in East City upon being transferred to Central City. Despite Havoc's initial fears regarding Cathrine's family bloodline, he finds himself attracted to her and the two appear to get along well. But when he asks Cathrine out directly, she apologizes and says that she only dates muscular men who are like her brother Alex Louis Armstrong, much to Havoc's shock and disappointment.

Cathrine appears again in Chapter 83 when Olivier Mira Armstrong demands that Philip Gargantos Armstrong leave the Armstrong mansion to her and immediately vacate. He understands that his daughter is warning him of some impending danger and does so, leaving for Xing with Mrs. Armstrong and Cathrine. As Olivier and Alex battle over the right to the mansion, Cathrine kindly offers wishes of good luck to Alex, suggesting that she may prefer her brother to her eldest sister.

In Episode 38 of the 2009 anime series, Cathrine is revealed to have thwarted Yoki's attempt to rob the Armstrong mansion when he accidentally startled her by running into the same room and she threw her piano straight at him in a fit of terror.

Etymology

The name Katherine is of Greek origin and has numerous debated origins, one of which is καθαρός (katharos), meaning "pure, clear" in Greek. 
Cathrine is a form of the name primarily used in Scandinavian countries, such as Norway, Denmark, and Sweden.
Catherine is an alternate spelling of the same name originating from French. 
Cathrine's middle name Elle means "she" in French. 

Trivia

Rie Kugimiya (Cathrine's seiyu) also voices Alphonse Elric in the anime, films and three of the FMA video games.
In Episode 37, Cathrine explains to Jean Havoc that she likes men who have "a more muscular physique like my brother's." and prefers "good-looking men."
Unlike Olivier Mira Armstrong, who was sharp eyes, Cathrine has droopy eyes.
